content.full_text:
California
Fair POlitical
Practices Cornrnission
Marsha Loveridge
459o University Ave.
Riverside, CA 92501-
January 18, 19 91
Re: Your Request
Ass istance
Our File No.
For Informal
I-90-706
Dear Ms. Loveridge:
You have requested advice concerning the conflict of interest
disclosure provisions of the PoliticaL Reform Act.1/ 
Because you
have not indicated that you are seeking advice on behalf of a
person who has duties under the Act, we are treating your letter
as a request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation
1,8329 (c) (4) (copy enclosed. ) 2/
OUESTION
What is the proper procedure for reporting a gift that is
given away or returned?
CONCLUSION
with the exception of passes or season tickets for adrnission
to or access to facilities 
such as theaters or amusement parks, an
official 
must disclose receipt of a gift unless the donor is
reirnbursed for the gift or the gift is returned, unused, to the
1/Go.r"..,a"r,t code Section 81ooo-91015. AII statutory references
are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 california Code of
Regulations Section 1800o, et seq. A11 references to
regulations are to Title 2, Di-vision 6 of the california code of
Regulations.
2/Irrfo.^ul assistance d.oes not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or forrnal written advice.
(Regulation 18329 (c) (3). )
.128 J Strcct, Suite tit'tt\ o P.o. Box tio? O Sircr:rmcnrtt C..\ 95sLr+-Otlt)l r 1916'1 l2l-5660


Ms. M. Loveridge
January 18, 1991
Page 2
donor, or is donated, unused, to a charitable organization or a
government aqrency without claiming any deduction for tax purposes.
The rei:nbursement, return or donation must occur within 30 days of
receipt. 
A gift which is not reimbursed and which is retained by
the official 
is deened to have been accepted and rnust be disclosed
even if the official 
discards or does not use the gift or gives
the gift to another person.
A pass or season ticket for admission to or access to
facilities 
(such as passes to theaters) has no value if the
official 
receiving the pass or season ticket does not use it and
does not give the pass or season ticket to another person.
DIECUAAION
Regulation 18726.1(a) (copy enclosed) states that a gift is
provided to, received by, or promised to an official 
at the tirne
the offer j-s extended if the gift is offered to the off icial- in
such a manner that it is within the official,s 
discretion whether
or not to accept it and the official 
ultirnately decides to accept
the gift. 
A gift is deemed accepted if it is tendered and comes
under the control of the official 
and the official 
and does not
return j.t, donate it, or reirnburse the donor for it as provided
beIow. Discarding or otherwise turning a gift over to another
person constitutes acceptance of the gift by the official.
Regulation L8726.1(b) provides that an official 
has not
received a gift if the official 
returns, donates, or reimburses
the donor in the following manner:
(1) The official 
returns the gift, 
unused, within 30 days of
receipt, to the donor or his or her agent or intermediary.
(2) The official 
donates the gift, 
unused, within 30 days of
receipt, to a charitable organization without claiming it as
a charitable contribution for tax purposes.
(3) The official 
donates the gift, 
unused, within 30 days of
receipt, to a state, local or federal government agency
without claining any deduction for tax purposes.
(4) The officiaL reirnburses the donor or his or her agent or
interrnediary for the gift within 30 days of receipt.
Per your request, enclosed is a copy of Regulation L8'726-3,
which provides that for purposes of disclosure, the value of a
pass oi season ticket for adrnission to or access to facilities 
is


Ms. M Loveridge
January 18, 1991
Page 3
the fair market value of the actual use by the official, 
including
any guests accompanying the official, 
plus the fair rnarket value
of any possible use by any person or persons to rrhom the official
transfers the privilege or use of the pass or season ticket.
If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact me at (91-6) 322-5662.
S incerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting GeneraL counsel
QolC^-'ruffi-
by: colleen Mccee
Political 
Reform consultant
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division
Enclosures
